title: Flora & Co. Instagram Strategy
role: Social Media Manager, Content Writer, Graphic Designer
goal: Launch and grow an Instagram presence for a new botanical lifestyle brand entering a competitive market, establishing a distinctive visual identity and brand voice from day one.
audience: Women 25–40 interested in sustainable living, botanical aesthetics, and considered home design. Early adopter profile — seeks brands with an authentic point of view.
deliverables: Monthly analytics reports with strategic notes
learnings: Illustrated carousel content with a clear educational structure consistently outperformed photography by 3× on saves — original art is a genuine differentiator in a photography-saturated niche.
Community reshares and UGC prompts drove more sustained follower growth than hashtag strategy — building explicit prompts into the content calendar was high-impact.
Posting at 7–9am on weekdays reached a significantly higher save rate than evening posting for this audience; A/B testing scheduling assumptions early was worth the time.

title: Luna Collective Ongoing Social
goal: Develop and execute an ongoing content strategy for an independent wellness brand centred on moon cycle practices, growing an engaged community from a small base.
audience: Adults 20–35 interested in wellness, intentional living, and spiritual practice. High engagement audience — prefers depth over volume.
deliverables: Quarterly strategy reviews with competitor audit
learnings: Tying content to real astronomical dates (actual moon phases, not generic scheduling) created anticipation and higher return-visit rates from core followers — the calendar structure itself became a retention mechanic.
Long-form educational carousel posts drove the highest saves of any format — this audience is readers, not scrollers.
Story engagement was disproportionately high; shifting resources toward more story output was one of the highest-ROI strategy changes of the engagement.

title: Sage Studio Launch Campaign
goal: Build pre-launch buzz and convert Instagram followers into waitlist sign-ups for an independent art education platform launching to early access.
audience: Beginner and intermediate visual artists, 18–35. Highly motivated learners who follow artist accounts for education, not just inspiration.
pillars: Waitlist CTA with clear value prop
deliverables: Landing page headline and body copy
learnings: Founder-led content (face on camera, personal narrative) consistently outperformed polished graphic posts by a wide margin — authenticity is not a nice-to-have in this niche.
Specific benefit CTAs ("join the waitlist for early access to X module") significantly outperformed generic "sign up now" calls to action — the more specific the promise, the higher the conversion.
Weekly story polls about curriculum preferences doubled as audience research and boosted engagement in one move — that kind of dual-purpose content became a template for the whole campaign.

title: Meridian Press Social Revitalisation
goal: Revitalise dormant social media accounts for an independent literary publisher and build an engaged reader community around their catalogue.
audience: Literary fiction readers, 28–55. Highly literate, trust-sensitive audience that responds poorly to overt marketing and strongly to genuine enthusiasm.
learnings: Quote graphics from the books — well-chosen, typeset simply — generated the highest share rates of any format; readable, extractable value travels.
Author interview content (even short Q&A graphics) drove the most meaningful DM conversations with potential readers and buyers.
Consistency beat volume consistently: posting 4 times per week outperformed 7 times per week on every engagement metric. Quality of attention matters more than quantity of posts.

title: Nova Apparel Content Strategy
goal: Create a distinctive illustrated visual identity for a streetwear brand's social channels and drive engagement around product drops.
audience: Young adults 18–28 interested in streetwear, independent art, and youth culture. High aesthetic standards; brand loyalty is earned through creative credibility.
learnings: Original illustrated content significantly outperformed product photography for this brand and audience — creative differentiation is the actual competitive advantage here.
Drop reveal story sequences with countdown stickers drove 3× the traffic of standard story posts; anticipation mechanics are effective when the product has genuine appeal.
Caption length was inversely correlated with engagement for this audience — shorter, punchier, often one line. Reading habits differ sharply between niches.

title: Verdant Zero Sustainability Campaign
goal: Run a focused 30-day awareness campaign for a sustainability product launch, using educational carousel content to build credibility and drive conversions.
audience: Eco-conscious consumers, 25–45. Highly information-literate; fact-checks claims; distrusts greenwashing. Trust must be earned through transparency.
learnings: Educational carousel content with a strict 'one fact per slide' structure achieved the highest completion rates — complexity presented simply outperforms simplified content every time.
This audience fact-checks. In-post source citations were not optional; they were the difference between shares and silence. Cite everything.
Partnering with micro-influencers in the sustainability niche (under 50K followers) drove more targeted engagement and more genuine conversations than broader reach options.
